title: A* Grid Path Planner for Differential Drive Robot
description: Mobile robot navigating an 8x8 grid using A* greedy replanning, 5-sensor IR wall detection, L298N differential drive, I2C LCD status display, and Bluetooth goal commands with out-of-bounds fault halt.
objective: Navigate from start to BT-commanded goal; replan on new wall detections; log path at 2 Hz; halt on OOB fault.
constraints: Arduino Mega; 5x TCRT5000 on A0-A4; L298N; HC-05 BT on Serial1; 9 V battery pack.
supporting_fact_titles: Differential Drive Kinematics and Turning Radius

element_usage_notes.ICM-42688-P High-Rate IMU via SPI: Provides yaw heading to resolve turn direction for A* next-cell commands.
element_usage_notes.5-Channel TCRT5000 IR Wall Sensor Array: Builds 4-bit wall bitmap used by astar_next_cell each scan cycle.
element_usage_notes.L298N Dual H-Bridge Differential Drive: Executes forward/turn motion from A* cell direction output.
element_usage_notes.20x4 I2C LCD Navigation Display: Shows current cell, goal, and heading after each move.
element_usage_notes.HC-05 Bluetooth Goal Command Link: Receives 'G x,y' goal commands; streams JSON pose at 2 Hz.

element_interactions.description: Wall bitmap fed to astar_next_cell; output drives L298N motors.
IMU heading ensures accurate turns to A* next cell.
New goal triggers fresh wall scan and replanning cycle.
Current position and wall status shown on LCD after each move.
